refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context inputs, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) baseline recording with traceability, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding documented performance targets and maintained baselines/calibration updates. High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance targets are automatically computed from worksite inputs (mission profile, terrain, payload, safety plan); (b) each target is traceable to source worksite input; (c) baseline snapshots are recorded with timestamp and operator identity; (d) all updates (requirement changes, calibration adjustments) are logged with reason and approval; (e) history is queryable and auditable.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle profile, safety constraints) from Site Manager input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirement targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record and baseline performance requirements with full traceability to source inputs and derivation logic.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain managed history of requirement baselines and calibration updates with audit trail (timestamp, operator, reason, approval).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide query and reporting interface for performance requirement baselines and change history to Site Manager and Safety Manager.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Requirements Engine (PRE) module that ingests mission context via API, executes requirement derivation algorithms, persists baselines and history in a dedicated database, and exposes query/reporting APIs.
DP-B: Distributed requirement derivation where each Vehicle computes its own performance targets locally based on mission context broadcast, with periodic synchronization to a central history log.
DP-C: Manual requirement entry by Site Manager with system-assisted validation and history tracking, no automatic derivation.

DP-D: Hybrid approach: PRE module handles automatic derivation and baseline management; Vehicle modules cache derived targets locally for real-time reference; Teleoperation module queries PRE for requirement updates during remote operation.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Requirements Engine (PRE) module that ingests mission context via API, executes requirement derivation algorithms, persists baselines and history in a dedicated database, and exposes query/reporting APIs.

potential_sources: PRE must receive mission context inputs (worksite inputs, safety plan) from Site Manager interface—this is a necessary input coupling, not a design flaw.
PRE must expose requirement baselines to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules via Tasking interface—this is a necessary output coupling for operational use.
PRE must log SafetyAlert events and safety-critical requirement changes—this is a necessary safety coupling.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is minimal and necessary. PRE is decoupled from Vehicle control logic and Teleoperation UI, reducing circular dependencies. DP-A avoids the distributed inconsistency risk of DP-B and the manual overhead of DP-C.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Mission context inputs (terrain, payload, cycle profile, safety constraints) are available and well-defined at the time of requirement derivation.
ASSUMPTION: Requirement derivation algorithms are deterministic and can be validated offline.

RISK: If mission context is incomplete or changes frequently, derived requirements may become stale; mitigation: implement real-time context monitoring and trigger re-derivation on significant changes.
RISK: Requirement derivation algorithms may be complex and difficult to validate; mitigation: use model-based design and formal verification for safety-critical margins.
RISK: Database performance may degrade with large history volumes; mitigation: implement archival and indexing strategies.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: None at this stage. DP-A is well-justified and decoupled. Proceed to IM assignment.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module owns the Performance Requirements Engine (PRE) as a core subsystem. PRE ingests mission context, executes requirement derivation algorithms, manages baseline and history persistence, and exposes requirement data to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules via standardized interfaces.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: PRE outputs derived performance requirement baselines (cycle time targets, energy efficiency targets, availability targets, safety margins) to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules via Tasking interface. Format: structured requirement objects with traceability metadata.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: PRE receives mission context telemetry from Vehicle and Site Manager inputs (terrain data, payload, cycle profile, safety plan updates) via Telemetry interface. Format: mission context data structures.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: PRE logs safety-critical requirement changes and receives safety constraint updates via SafetyAlert interface. PRE may emit alerts if derived safety margins fall below thresholds. Format: safety event records.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: PRE communicates with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules over Network Link for requirement distribution and history queries. Format: API calls and responses.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level container and natural owner of the Performance Requirements Engine. PRE is a core system service that derives requirements for all operational modes (autonomous, teleoperated). Minimal diffusion: PRE logic is centralized; only requirement outputs are distributed to Vehicle and Teleoperation.

Vehicle module is responsible for executing tasks, not deriving system-level performance requirements. Placing PRE in Vehicle would create tight coupling between requirement derivation and vehicle control, and would require replication across multiple vehicles. High diffusion risk.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operation UI and control, not requirement derivation. PRE in Teleoperation would couple requirement logic to UI, limiting reuse in autonomous mode. High diffusion risk.

Safety Services may own safety constraint validation, but requirement derivation (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) is broader than safety. Placing PRE in Safety Services would fragment requirement logic. Moderate diffusion risk.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Performance Requirements Engine is a system-level service that must be centralized, accessible to all operational modes (Vehicle autonomous, Teleoperation remote), and decoupled from vehicle control and UI logic. NGHE Solution is the only module with sufficient scope and neutrality to own PRE without creating coupling or diffusion.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution module is not yet fully defined or has unclear boundaries, PRE ownership may be ambiguous. MITIGATION: Clarify NGHE Solution scope as the system-level container for all core services.

RISK: PRE may require real-time performance (low-latency requirement updates during operation). If NGHE Solution is deployed remotely or has high latency, PRE responsiveness may suffer. MITIGATION: Implement caching in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; PRE is authoritative but not on the critical path for real-time control.

RISK: PRE database may grow large and require maintenance. If NGHE Solution does not have adequate storage and backup infrastructure, history management may fail. MITIGATION: Allocate storage and backup resources to NGHE Solution; implement archival policies.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: None. NGHE Solution is the correct owner. Proceed to gate review.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (Centralized PRE) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) ingests mission context via API, (2) computes performance targets using algorithms, (3) records baselines with traceability, (4) maintains managed history with audit trail, (5) exposes query/reporting APIs. DP-A is decoupled, scalable, and avoids the inconsistency risks of distributed approaches. No design flaws detected.

diffusion_risks: LOW RISK: PRE outputs (requirement baselines) are distributed to Vehicle and Teleoperation via Tasking interface. This is necessary and expected; outputs are read-only from consumer perspective, minimizing coupling.
LOW RISK: PRE inputs (mission context) are ingested from multiple sources (Site Manager, Vehicle telemetry). This is necessary for requirement derivation; inputs are well-defined and versioned.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution ownership is justified. PRE is a system-level service, not a vehicle-specific or UI-specific function. Centralization in NGHE Solution avoids duplication and ensures consistency. No diffusion detected.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH. Verification of CR-NGHE-005 can be performed by: (a) unit testing of requirement derivation algorithms against known mission contexts, (b) integration testing of PRE with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, (c) audit trail inspection for baseline and update records, (d) traceability matrix linking requirements to source inputs. All verification activities are feasible within NGHE Solution scope.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: CLEAR. CR-NGHE-005 is allocated to NGHE Solution module. Sub-FRs are allocated to PRE subsystem within NGHE Solution. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are clearly defined. No ambiguity in allocation.

recommended_revisions: REVISION 1: Clarify the definition of 'mission context' in CR-NGHE-005.1. Specify which worksite inputs (terrain, payload, cycle profile, safety constraints) are mandatory vs. optional, and define default values if inputs are missing.

REVISION 2: Define the requirement derivation algorithms in CR-NGHE-005.2 at a higher level (e.g., cycle time = mission distance / vehicle speed; energy efficiency = mission energy / mission distance). Detailed algorithm design should be deferred to detailed design phase.

REVISION 3: Specify the audit trail format and retention policy in CR-NGHE-005.4. Define who can approve requirement changes (Site Manager, Safety Manager, System Engineer) and what approval workflow is required.

REVISION 4: Add a sub-FR for requirement validation: CR-NGHE-005.6: Validate derived performance requirements against vehicle capabilities and safety constraints; alert Site Manager and Safety Manager if requirements are infeasible.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 is not atomic and requires further decomposition. The refined FR has five sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5), each of which requires its own FR-DP-IM analysis. The selected DP (Centralized PRE) is sound and well-justified, and the IM assignment to NGHE Solution is correct. However, the sub-FRs must be decomposed to the next level to define the detailed design of PRE (mission context ingestion, requirement derivation algorithms, baseline recording, history management, query/reporting). Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to achieve atomic FRs and detailed design parameters.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle profile, safety constraints) from Site Manager input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirement targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record and baseline performance requirements with full traceability to source inputs and derivation logic.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain managed history of requirement baselines and calibration updates with audit trail (timestamp, operator, reason, approval).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide query and reporting interface for performance requirement baselines and change history to Site Manager and Safety Manager.

assumptions: Mission context inputs (terrain, payload, cycle profile, safety constraints) are available and well-defined at the time of requirement derivation.
Requirement derivation algorithms are deterministic and can be validated offline.
NGHE Solution module is the system-level container with sufficient scope to own the Performance Requirements Engine.
Vehicle and Teleoperation modules are consumers of derived requirements and do not participate in requirement derivation.
Site Manager and Safety Manager are authorized to approve requirement changes and have access to query/reporting interfaces.
open_risks: Mission context may be incomplete or change frequently, causing derived requirements to become stale. Mitigation: implement real-time context monitoring and trigger re-derivation on significant changes.
Requirement derivation algorithms may be complex and difficult to validate. Mitigation: use model-based design and formal verification for safety-critical margins.
PRE database may grow large and require maintenance. Mitigation: implement archival and indexing strategies.
PRE may require real-time performance (low-latency requirement updates). Mitigation: implement caching in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; PRE is authoritative but not on the critical path.
Requirement validation against vehicle capabilities may reveal infeasible requirements. Mitigation: add validation sub-FR and alert mechanism.
evidence_needed: Definition of mission context data model (terrain, payload, cycle profile, safety constraints) with mandatory and optional fields.
Specification of requirement derivation algorithms (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) with mathematical formulas or pseudocode.
Definition of baseline recording format and traceability metadata (source inputs, derivation logic, timestamp, operator).
Specification of audit trail format and retention policy (who can approve, what approval workflow).
Definition of query/reporting interface (API, data formats, access control).
Validation criteria for requirement feasibility (vehicle capabilities, safety constraints).
Performance requirements for PRE (latency, throughput, availability) to ensure operational suitability.
